s2.q: Which of the following is the main source of amniotic fluid during the first half of pregnancy?
s2.explain: In the first half of pregnancy, amniotic fluid is produced from maternal circulation. In the second half, fetal urination becomes the main source.

s2.q: What is the primary route of amniotic fluid resorption during the second half of pregnancy?
s2.explain: Fetal swallowing is the main route of amniotic fluid clearance during the second half of pregnancy.

s2.q: Which of the following is NOT a function of amniotic fluid?
s2.explain: Amniotic fluid surrounds and protects the fetus, regulates temperature, and allows fetal movement. Nutrient supply is through the placenta.

s2.q: In obstetric practice, this knowledge helps:
s2.explain: Comprehensive knowledge enables prevention, early diagnosis, and optimal management.

s2.q: The clinical significance of this section is:
options: It applies only to research settings
s2.explain: Understanding these concepts directly informs clinical management.

s3.q: Polyhydramnios is defined as an amniotic fluid volume (AFV) greater than:
s3.explain: Polyhydramnios is defined as AFV > 2,000 mL. Oligohydramnios is defined as AFV < 500 mL.

s3.q: Which is the most common cause of both polyhydramnios and oligohydramnios?
s3.explain: Idiopathic (unknown cause) is the most common etiology for both polyhydramnios and oligohydramnios.

s3.q: A patient has fundal height more than expected, difficult palpation and difficult FHS auscultation. Which condition is most likely?
s3.explain: Classic polyhydramnios: uterus larger than dates, difficult palpation and auscultation of fetal parts and heart sounds.

s3.q: Which fetal malformation is associated with polyhydramnios?
s3.explain: Fetal esophageal and duodenal atresia prevent fetal swallowing, leading to polyhydramnios. Renal agenesis causes oligohydramnios.

s3.q: Which of the following is a key concept?
options: It is essential for understanding the condition
It is rarely relevant in clinical practice
It applies only to specific populations
It has been replaced by newer classifications
s3.explain: Understanding key concepts is fundamental to clinical management.

s4.q: Using Single Deepest Pocket (SDP), oligohydramnios is diagnosed when SDP is:
Using the Amniotic Fluid Index (AFI), polyhydramnios is defined as:
s4.explain: AFI > 25 cm = polyhydramnios. AFI 10-20 cm = normal. AFI < 5 cm = oligohydramnios.

s4.q: The Amniotic Fluid Index (AFI) uses the:
s4.explain: AFI uses the 4-quadrant method: the deepest vertical amniotic pocket in each quadrant is measured and summed.

s4.q: When assessing this aspect, the most important factor is:
s4.explain: Assessment requires integration of clinical, laboratory, and imaging findings.

s4.q: In obstetric practice, this knowledge helps:
s4.explain: Comprehensive knowledge enables prevention, early diagnosis, and optimal management.

s5.q: Which complication is specific to oligohydramnios and a major cause of neonatal mortality when prolonged?
s5.explain: Pulmonary hypoplasia is a major complication of prolonged oligohydramnios as fetal lung development is impaired.

s5.q: Which complication is shared by both polyhydramnios and oligohydramnios?
s5.explain: Both polyhydramnios and oligohydramnios share preterm labor and abnormal fetal presentation as common complications.

s5.q: Umbilical cord prolapse is more commonly associated with:
s5.explain: Umbilical cord prolapse is a complication of polyhydramnios, especially after sudden decompression following ROM.
Fetal well-being is closely linked to maternal condition — multiple fetal complications are possible.

s5.q: Which of the following is a key concept?
options: It is essential for understanding the condition
It is rarely relevant in clinical practice
It applies only to specific populations
It has been replaced by newer classifications
s5.explain: Understanding key concepts is fundamental to clinical management.

s6.q: Which is a management option for polyhydramnios?
s6.explain: Amnio-reduction (drainage of excess fluid) is used for polyhydramnios. Amnio-infusion is for oligohydramnios.

s6.q: A post-term pregnancy with oligohydramnios should be managed by:
s6.explain: When the pregnancy is post-term and AF is low, delivery rather than watchful waiting is indicated.

s6.q: Which medication can be used in managing polyhydramnios?
s6.explain: A prostaglandin synthetase inhibitor (e.g., indomethacin) can reduce amniotic fluid production in polyhydramnios.

s6.q: Which of the following is a key concept?
options: It is essential for understanding the condition
It is rarely relevant in clinical practice
It applies only to specific populations
It has been replaced by newer classifications
s6.explain: Understanding key concepts is fundamental to clinical management.

s6.q: The clinical significance of this section is:
options: It applies only to research settings
s6.explain: Understanding these concepts directly informs clinical management.
